raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Accurate data is critical to addressing California’s homelessness crisis.",
    "A recent report by the Terner Center for Housing Innovation and All Home showed a severe shortage of extremely low-income (ELI) housing in California.",
    "The extremely low-income population is particularly vulnerable to homelessness.",
    "Improving data collection on ELI housing production will help the state identify future policy and funding needs.",
    "Most people with ELI in the Bay Area receive no housing subsidies or supports.",
    "The supply of homes affordable to extremely low-income individuals is vastly inadequate.",
    "Many individuals with extremely low income are one financial emergency away from homelessness.",
    "About half of all people with extremely low incomes are children or seniors.",
    "Almost half of working-age adults with extremely low income are doing essential jobs.",
    "Some communities are already benefiting from examining their ELI data.",
    "The City of San Jose and County of Santa Clara track data on ELI housing production and allocate resources accordingly.",
    "A lack of housing affordable to extremely low-income households is a main driver of homelessness.",
    "Existing reporting laws do not provide data to track housing production for extremely low-income Californians.",
    "The bill addresses critical gaps in data that will improve the ability to plan for and build ELI housing in California."
  ]
}
```
